body_txt: In Real Life , dreams are just dreams . If a friend turns on you or your sister dies or you're living in a palace made of lego that floats in the sky and changes colour, it's generally not an early glimpse of things to come . On the other hand, works of fiction have a strange attitude towards prophetic dreams, whereby it's not necessary to have any actual Psychic Powers to have them. You can just be a super-warrior (as in Buffy the Vampire Slayer ) or a healer (as in Carnivàle ), and all your dreams about the past, present and/or future will come true/turn out to be true, down to the smallest detail. In fact, it's not even necessary to have any powers of any description, or be in a show where supernatural things occur. Stranger still, all of these dreams will be the otherwise-rare sort to have no surreal elements of any kind. May be an example of Mundane Fantastic or Skepticism Failure . Subtrope of Dreaming of Things to Come .
